title: Sid Meier’s Civilization VI (289070)
text:
# Compatibility Report
- Name of the game with compatibility issues: Sid Meier’s Civilization VI
- Steam AppID of the game: 289070

## System Information
- GPU: GTX 1060 6GB
- Driver/LLVM version: 450.56.01
- Kernel version: 5.7.8-zen2-1-zen
- Link to full system information report as [Gist](https://gist.github.com/):  https://gist.github.com/TheGreatestJannet/71096dddbd11463b0d28c3f1891fc903
- Proton version: 5.0-9

## I confirm:
- [X] that I haven't found an existing compatibility report for this game.
- [X] that I have checked whether there are updates for my system available.



## Symptoms 
Game gets stuck on a grey screen.

## Reproduction
Launch the game.

## Additional Information
Seems to be related to this: https://bugs.winehq.org/show_bug.cgi?id=49315

title: GTFO (493520)
text:
GTFO (493520)

Issue transferred from https://github.com/ValveSoftware/Proton/issues/9176.
@HubsChromeFreeze posted on 2025-11-03T02:51:27:

# Compatibility Report
- GTFO
- 493520

## System Information
- GPU: NVIDIA GeForce RTX 3050 Ti Laptop GPU
- Video driver version: 535.247.01
- Kernel version: 6.1.0-40-amd64
- Link to full system information report as [Gist]: https://gist.github.com/HubsChromeFreeze/855b9ba07c74041c69ea19320b5ceace
- NOTE Regarding System Info: Steam (and other programs) do not read system information correctly and won't properly find the NVIDIA card.  Games are launched on the system by putting _NV_PRIME_RENDER_OFFLOAD=1 __GLX_VENDOR_LIBRARY_NAME=nvidia %command% into the launch options.
- Proton version: Attempted Hotfix, Experimental, 9.0-4, 8.0-5, 7.0-6, 6.3-8, 5.13-6, 10.0-2 (beta), GE-Proton10-25 to varying results; none are functional.

## I confirm:
- [x] that I haven't found an existing compatibility report for this game.
- [x] that I have checked whether there are updates for my system available.

## Proton Log:

https://gist.github.com/HubsChromeFreeze/95fa4109fa718ff7637426ba64e8f9e5
Proton logs do not appear in the provided location by the template.  This could be because debian-installation has put it in a bad spot where it's hard to find, or it is Proton itself crashing and generates this report and no log.

## Symptoms
Using any of the above Proton will yield different results;
For 9.0-4, the game will crash during the startup sequence, right around as the cutscene begins.  No menus besides the EULA agreement screen are reached.
For more experimental versions, the game often will not launch at all, or produces an identical result, kicking to the Unity Crash Handler.
For older versions, down to 5.13-6, the game will either crash immediately -- or in the case of Proton 6.3-8, will NOT crash at the described location and will instead cause the game to freeze as the menu cutscene says "Waiting for system assets to initialize.." (exact number of periods too), not loading and not crashing.  This is as far as I have been able to reach.

## Reproduction
Launch the game with hardware and software as described.
Notes: PROTON_USE_WINED3D=1 causes the game under MOST Proton versions (if it does not crash) to not successfully load (or at least load so slowly my patience ran out -- so about 5-10 minutes).  A long list (which I cannot remember, unfortunately) of other launch options have been tried, to no positive effect.


Edit: Fixed links.

title: FOUNDRY (983870)
text:
# Compatibility Report
- Name of the game with compatibility issues: Foundry
- Steam AppID of the game: 983870

## System Information
- GPU: Radeon RX 6950XT
- Video driver version: Mesa 24.1.0-rc1
- Kernel version: 6.8.7-201.fsync.fc39.x86_64
- Link to full system information report as [Gist](https://gist.github.com/): https://gist.github.com/omega9380/00ce8116175b51b6810fd63883e29e4a
- Proton version: proton-9.0-1

## I confirm:
- [ x ] that I haven't found an existing compatibility report for this game.
- [ x ] that I have checked whether there are updates for my system available.


[steam-983870.log](https://github.com/ValveSoftware/Proton/files/15367260/steam-983870.log)

## Symptoms 
When running under Proton 9.0-1, I am unable to connect to any multiplayer servers.  When attempting to connect to public servers, dedicated servers, shared games from friends, or direct IP connections, it will hang at "Sending join request".  On the dedicated server I attempted to connect to, the logs showed a connection but it would time out.

Running the game under Proton 8.0-5 allows multiplayer connections to work, however the graphics are terrible.  The framerate drops below 50fps, textures aren't shown correctly, and the clouds are pixelated.  Running under 9.0-1, I can get 144+ fps on max settings (but can't connect to multiplayer!)

## Reproduction
Run the game on any version of Proton above 8.0-5 and attempt to connect to any type of multiplayer server.

title: Ninki Seiyuu: How to Make a Pop Voice Actress (1777750)
text:
# Compatibility Report
- Name of the game with compatibility issues:Ninki Seiyuu: How to Make a Pop Voice Actress
- Steam AppID of the game:1777750

## System Information
- GPU: AMD Vega 56/64
- Driver/LLVM version: Mesa 22.0.5
- Kernel version: 5.19.1
- Proton version:7.0-3

## I confirm:
- [x] that I haven't found an existing compatibility report for this game.
- [x] that I have checked whether there are updates for my system available.


## Symptoms 
After starting, game show error dialog (pop-up) and closes after pressing ok.

![Screenshot_20220815_194204](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/757407/184678867-b3b5ac03-1108-4f1b-8f62-fa58ef0b1d20.png)

## Reproduction
1. start the game
2. select language
3. observe the error

[steam-1777750.log.gz](https://github.com/ValveSoftware/Proton/files/9338614/steam-1777750.log.gz)
